text: How do we measure angles? There are two main systems: the Sexagesimal system and the Circular system.
In the Sexagesimal system, we use degrees, minutes, and seconds. One degree is 60 minutes, and one minute is 60 seconds.
In the Circular system, we use Radians. One radian is the angle subtended by an arc whose length equals the radius.
We convert between these systems using the relation: pi radians equals 180 degrees. To convert degrees to radians, multiply by pi over 180.
For example, converting 30 degrees gives us pi over 6 radians.
